title: Production Server Disk Full Causing Service Failures
symptoms: 500 Internal Server Error responses from app-prod-web-07
Disk utilization at 100% on the root volume
Application unable to write to /tmp directory
Log files consuming 95% of disk space with debug-level entries
Health check endpoint still returning 200 OK despite failures
root_cause: Debug logging accidentally left enabled on production server app-prod-web-07 after a troubleshooting session, generating 180GB of verbose log data in 72 hours
resolution: Cleared stale log files, disabled debug logging, resized the EBS volume from 200GB to 500GB, and updated health check to verify disk space availability
preventive_actions: Implement automated log rotation with size-based limits
Add disk utilization alerting at 80% and 90% thresholds
Update health check endpoints to include disk space verification
Create automated process to detect and disable debug logging in production
Standardize EBS volume sizing with growth capacity planning

timeline.event: Application begins returning 500 errors for requests hitting this server
Alert fires for elevated 5xx error rate on the load balancer
Lisa Park identifies app-prod-web-07 as the source of errors
Disk full condition confirmed, debug logging identified as cause
Server removed from load balancer, stale logs cleared
Debug logging disabled, EBS volume resize initiated
Server returned to load balancer pool, incident resolved

embedding_summary: Production server app-prod-web-07 reached 100% disk utilization due to debug logging accidentally left enabled after troubleshooting. 12.5% of web traffic affected for 22 minutes with 500 errors. Resolved by clearing logs, disabling debug mode, and resizing the EBS volume.
